key_insight: An ensemble deep-learning pipeline was built to predict lethal outcomes after acute myocardial infarction and identify the biomarkers most associated with severe complications.
clinical_relevance: If validated, this approach could support faster risk stratification and more consistent early decision-making for patients with MI.
method: Preprocessed clinical data with missing-value handling and imbalance correction using SVMSMOTE, ADASYN, and class-weighted methods.
Selected important features using wrapper and embedded methods, then standardized inputs for modeling.
Combined Logistic Regression, Random Forest, LightGBM, Bagging SVM, and an artificial neural network to improve prediction performance.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports model evaluation with precision, recall, and other performance metrics to compare methods and identify the best clinical candidate.
watch_item: The abstract does not provide external validation or deployment details, so real-world robustness and generalizability remain uncertain.
paper_title: Predicting Lethal Outcome (Cause) And Understanding Key Biomarkers Linked With Acute Myocardial Infarction Using Deep Artificial Neural Network And Ensemble Of Machine Learning Methodologies
